events.title: Musical Potluck
events.description: Join us for an afternoon of music, followed by a potluck supper. Musicians of all ages and skill levels are invited to participate, with opportunities to play in both small and large groups. Our goal is to bring together Oberlin’s vibrant musical community for a fun and welcoming experience. If you don’t play an instrument, you’re still eagerly invited—come relax and enjoy a wide variety of live music across genres. Please RSVP.

events.title: OCTaiko Final Show/Study Break
events.description: Need a break from studying? Come see OCTaiko's Final Show/Study Break! Featuring a showcase by our Intro to Taiko Drumming ExCo students. Rain location Hales Gymnasium.

events.title: That's What She Sang: Barbershop Quartet Recital
events.description: Come enjoy the vintage sound of a female barbershop quartet!

events.title: OFS Presents "The Fog of War" (2003)
events.description: The Oberlin Film Society (OFS) will screen The Fog of War (2003), a documentary directed by Errol Morris. Centered on former U.S. Secretary of Defense Robert McNamara, the film reflects on war, ethics, and political decision-making in the 20th century. Provocative and unsettling, it won the Academy Award for Best Documentary.
reviewFlags: Uncertain event type mapping: Films & Screenings

events.title: Nothing But Treble Final Concert
events.description: Join Oberlin's oldest women's and nonbinary a cappella group for our final concert! Featuring student arrangements of music by Charli XCX, Sammy Rae & the Friends, RAYE, Billie Eilish, and many more.

events.title: Tiny Ref Desk Concert: Abby & Rosalie

events.title: The Big Swap
events.description: The annual Big Swap returns on Tuesday, May 12 and runs through Monday, May 18. The Big Swap, a giant version of the campus Free Store, pops up during the last week of the academic year for students to drop unwanted clothes, school supplies, room essentials, etc. Everything can be taken for free by anyone in the campus or wider community.

events.title: Senior Recital: Max Lang, Musical Studies
events.description: A senior composition and voice recital presented by Max Lang in Fairchild Chapel.

events.title: Tuesday Tea / Bad Art? Engaging Students with the Unexpected and the Unconventional
events.description: How can we teach with art that refuses categorization? Fiona Giménez-Collins (OC 2025), Academic Programs Curatorial Assistant, will discuss sparking student dialogue around art objects that challenge typical modes of viewing. Delving into experiences of teaching with Mary Miss's Untitled (1975), Giménez-Collins will reflect on pushing students past discomfort and into new understandings of unconventional artworks.

events.title: OFS Presents "The Graduate" (1967)
events.description: The Oberlin Film Society (OFS) will screen The Graduate (1967), a comedy-drama directed by Mike Nichols. Starring Dustin Hoffman, the film captures post-college aimlessness and generational anxiety in 1960s America. With its iconic soundtrack and sharp humor, it helped launch the New Hollywood era.

events.title: Self-Guided Tour: Oberlin Origins
events.description: Move at your own pace through the Oberlin Heritage Center's 1866 Monroe House, exploring stories that outline Oberlin's founding, the college's promise of co-education, the community's commitment to abolition, and more. Tours are accessed through a provided tablet or your personal smart phone. Cost: $3/adult; free for college students and children under 18. Walk-ins are welcome. Last entry at 3:30 p.m. Call 440-774-1700 or visit www.oberlinheritagecenter.org to register.
reviewFlags: No confident postTypeId mapping; defaulted to [1].
Uncertain event type mapping: Commencement/Reunion Weekend
Uncertain event type mapping: Signature Programs

events.title: Guided Tour of the Jewett House by Fanny Jewett
events.description: Learn about Frank Fanning Jewett, Sarah Frances (Fanny) Gulick Jewett, Charles Martin Hall, the Aluminum Reduction Process and life in Oberlin during the Progressive Era on this 45-minute guided tour of the Oberlin Heritage Center’s 1884 Jewett House. Additional topics include architecture, interior design, social reform, and student boarders who stayed with the family during their time at Oberlin College. This experience includes a costumed living history portrayal of Fanny Jewett. Cost: $6/adult; $3/college student; and free for children under 18. Advance registration is required, and space is limited to 14 participants. Call 440-774-1700 or visit www.oberlinheritagecenter.org to register.

events.title: Tour: Freedom’s Friends: Abolition & the Underground Railroad History Walk
events.description: Prior to the Civil War, as many has 3,000 African Americans passed through or lived in Oberlin after escaping from slavery. The town was said to be second only to Canada as an asylum for freedom seekers. Guests on this guided history walk offered by the Oberlin Heritage Center will hear about the historic decision that shaped Oberlin’s growth as a station on the Underground Railroad and stories of the individuals and events that marked Oberlin as one of the most active abolitionist communities in Ohio. Cost: $10/adult; $5/college student; free for children under 18. Advance registration is required, and space is limited to 50 participants. Tour is weather dependent and may be cancelled by the Oberlin Heritage Center. Registrants may request a refund or transfer to another history walk offering if the tour is canceled. Call 440-774-1700 or visit www.oberlinheritagecenter.org to register.

events.title: Multifaith End-of-Year Ceremony
events.description: Students, faculty, staff, friends, and family are warmly invited to a joyful multifaith (including non-theistic) end-of-year ceremony as we send off the class of 2026. At this gathering, students will share readings and music that are meaningful to them, drawn from many traditions, cultures, and personal perspectives. Faculty will also offer letters of wisdom and encouragement as graduates prepare for what's next. This year, we are excited to hear letters written and read by Courtney-Savali Andrews, Raphael Jiménez, Greggor Mattson, John Petersen, and Ellen Wurtzel! Whether you are religious/spiritual or not, you are absolutely welcome here. This is a space for everyone to pause, reflect, and celebrate the community we've built together, and most especially the candidates for graduation. Family members, loved ones, and Oberlin community members encouraged to attend. Come as you are and join us for a ceremony of reflection and celebration.

events.title: Tour: Architecture History Walk
events.description: Enjoy a leisurely stroll on this 75-minute tour offered by the Oberlin Heritage Center which explores Oberlin’s architecture and the people who shaped the area around Tappan Square. This walk provides an opportunity to view an array of historic buildings that feature various styles including Gothic Modernist, Neo-Classical Revival, Romanesque, Late Victorian, and Queen Anne Revival. Cost: $10/adult; $5/college student; free for children under 18. Advance registration is required, and space is limited to 20 participants. Meet at the flagpoles on the southeast corner of Tappan Square, across from the Hotel at Oberlin. Tour is weather dependent and may be cancelled by the Oberlin Heritage Center. Registrants may request a refund or transfer to another history walk offering if the tour is canceled. Call 440-774-1700 or visit www.oberlinheritagecenter.org to register.
reviewFlags: No confident postTypeId mapping; defaulted to [1].
Uncertain event type mapping: Commencement/Reunion Weekend
Uncertain event type mapping: Signature Programs

events.title: Tour: Civil War to Civil Rights History Walk
events.description: This 90-minute tour offered by the Oberlin Heritage Center highlights historic events that reveal both Oberlin’s progress and setbacks in race relations dating from early Oberlin all the way up to the 21st Century. Learn how Oberlin was a leader in abolition, participating heavily in events such as the Oberlin-Wellington Rescue, the Raid on Harpers Ferry, and the American Civil War, but also struggled with its own issues of equality and racism during these early years and later through Reconstruction, Jim Crow, Civil Rights, and today. Cost: $10/adult; $5/college student; free for children under 18. Advance registration is required, and space is limited to 40 participants. Tour is weather dependent and may be cancelled by the Oberlin Heritage Center. Registrants may request a refund or transfer to another history walk offering if the tour is canceled. Call 440-774-1700 or visit www.oberlinheritagecenter.org to register.

events.title: Tour: One Step More: Oberlin Women’s History Walk
events.description: In 1834, John J. Shipherd pledged Oberlin’s commitment to “the elevation of female character” through education. Little did he know, there was a dynamite in that promise, and women carried the matches. This 75-minute walking tour offered by the Oberlin Heritage Center explores how powerful women made good on Shipherd’s promise and shaped what it meant to be a woman in Oberlin and beyond. This tour was developed by alumna Jen Graham ’12. Cost: $10/adult; $5/college student; free for children under 18. Advance registration is required, and space is limited to 20 participants. Meet at the flagpoles on the southeast corner of Tappan Square, across from the Hotel at Oberlin. Tour is weather dependent and may be cancelled by the Oberlin Heritage Center. Registrants may request a refund or transfer to another history walk offering if the tour is canceled. Call 440-774-1700 or visit www.oberlinheritagecenter.org to register.

events.title: Frank Lloyd Wright Open House: Commencement and Milestone Reunion Weekend
events.description: The Weltzheimer/Johnson House, designed by Frank Lloyd Wright, will be available for a walk-up open house. Presentations on the history of the home will be offered on the half hour. No advance tickets are required for this event and can be purchased at the door; cash only—$10 for adults (all funds raised contribute to the care of conservation of the house); OC students (with ID) and youths under 18 are free. PARKING: Parking is available on the south side of Morgan Street only (golf course side). Permission to park in this location for the open house is granted by the Oberlin Police Department. ARRIVAL: After parking, please follow the gravel path down and around the house to check in under the carport. ACCESSIBILITY: Please consult the Frank Lloyd Wright House page on the Allen Memorial Art Museum’s website for accessibility information or contact the AMAM Education Department if you have any questions.

events.title: Black Families Appreciation Ceremony
events.description: The Black Families Appreciation Ceremony is an annual pre-commencement ceremony and dinner. This annual event allows self-identifying Black graduates and allies the opportunity to express words of gratitude in front of their community and receive their kente cloth stole. This event is for graduating students, their families and friends, faculty and staff, and community supporters. Dinner is served immediately following the ceremony at Afrikan Heritage House. All are welcome to participate.

events.title: Oberlin College Commencement
events.description: We will honor the graduates of the Class of 2026 with a Commencement Ceremony on Tappan Square. To view live captions while attending the event in Tappan Square, visit go.oberlin.edu/captions on your mobile device. To watch the livestream, visit www.oberlin.edu/commencement/live. The event is outdoors and will take place rain or shine. No tickets are required.

events.title: Frank Lloyd Wright Open House / June-November 2026
events.description: Designed by Frank Lloyd Wright, the Weltzheimer/Johnson House is the first Usonian house in Ohio, and one of the few in the nation open to the public. Situated a mile and a half from the art museum at 534 Morgan Street in Oberlin, the house is open to the public the first Sunday of the month (with the exception of holiday weekends) from April to November with a docent presentation offered on the hour at noon, 1, 2, 3, and 4 p.m. Advance registration is required. Admission to the house is $10 per adult, free for OC college students with ID and anyone under 18. For tickets, parking information, and other details, see amam.oberlin.edu/flw. Make a day of it! The Allen Memorial Art Museum is open 1–5 on Sundays, and is always free. Plus, enjoy 10% off regular-priced menu items and merchandise at participating Oberlin businesses on the day of this event by showing your registration confirmation email. Get more details. Access Statement: Limited accessible parking is available near the back entrance to the home. Download a map to the accessibility parking at the house. This historic home is partially accessible to wheelchairs and fully accessible to most rolling walkers. The presentation space is wheelchair accessible and limited seating is available. The restroom is not wheelchair accessible, nor gender specific. Listening devices and ASL are currently not scheduled for this event, nor will it be recorded. If you are interested in touring the home outside of scheduled open houses, groups of 10 or more may make arrangements by contacting the Education Department at education.amam@oberlin.edu.

events.title: Thomas & Evon Cooper Competition 2026 - Violin: Round 1, Day 1 Session 1
events.description: Founded in 2010, the Thomas & Evon Cooper International Competition brings some of the world's most talented young musicians to the campus of Oberlin College and Conservatory. Alternating years feature a competition for violinists and a competition for pianists. Thursday, August 6 | Round 1, Day 1 Violinists perform 30-minute programs in Warner Concert Hall. 2:30-5:45 p.m. - Session 1 7:30-9:30 p.m. - Session 2 Friday, August 7 | Round 1, Day 2 Violinists perform 30-minute programs in Warner Concert Hall. 2:30-5:45 p.m. - Session 1 7:30-10:00 p.m. - Session 2 Announcement of the six advancing violinists follows the evening session. Saturday, August 8 | Round 2 Six violinists perform 45-minute recital programs in Warner Concert Hall. 3:00-6:15 p.m. - Session 1 8:00-9:30 p.m. - Session 2 Announcement of the three finalists follows the evening session. Sunday, August 9 | Honors Recital This evening's concert in Warner Concert Hall features outstanding performances selected by the Jury from the first three days of the Competition. 7:30 p.m. Honors Recital Monday, August 10 | Concerto Finals Three violinists perform complete concertos with the ProMusica Chamber Orchestra and David Danzmayr, conductor. 7:30 p.m. Concerto Finals | Warner Concert Hall

events.title: Frank Lloyd Wright Open House / June-November 2026
events.description: Designed by Frank Lloyd Wright, the Weltzheimer/Johnson House is the first Usonian house in Ohio, and one of the few in the nation open to the public. Situated a mile and a half from the art museum at 534 Morgan Street in Oberlin, the house is open to the public the first Sunday of the month (with the exception of holiday weekends) from April to November with a docent presentation offered on the hour at noon, 1, 2, 3, and 4 p.m. Advance registration is required. Admission to the house is $10 per adult, free for OC college students with ID and anyone under 18. For tickets, parking information, and other details, see amam.oberlin.edu/flw. Make a day of it! The Allen Memorial Art Museum is open 1–5 on Sundays, and is always free. Plus, enjoy 10% off regular-priced menu items and merchandise at participating Oberlin businesses on the day of this event by showing your registration confirmation email. Get more details. Access Statement: Limited accessible parking is available near the back entrance to the home. Download a map to the accessibility parking at the house. This historic home is partially accessible to wheelchairs and fully accessible to most rolling walkers. The presentation space is wheelchair accessible and limited seating is available. The restroom is not wheelchair accessible, nor gender specific. Listening devices and ASL are currently not scheduled for this event, nor will it be recorded. If you are interested in touring the home outside of scheduled open houses, groups of 10 or more may make arrangements by contacting the Education Department at education.amam@oberlin.edu.
